response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished senior web and distributed-systems engineer whose core expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not C++. His only documented C++ work is a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, neither of which involves the systems-level, multi-threaded, industrial-grade C++ this role demands. He has no evidence of experience with industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), secure development lifecycle standards (IEC 62443), or embedded/on-premises platform engineering, which are central to the position.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems and high-throughput engineering background (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O and low-latency design)
Proven event-driven architecture experience across multiple roles and projects (GISCollective, Sociomantic, Optilyz)
Solid software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, agile, and mentoring — aligning with the role's collaboration and quality expectations
concerns: C++ experience is minimal, dated (2014–2015), and not listed in his own skills section — a critical mismatch for a role requiring deep, production-grade C++ and STL expertise
No exposure to industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards that are core to this position
Entire career trajectory points toward web, cloud-native, and full-stack development rather than systems-level, on-premises, or embedded platform engineering
